- [P2] (seo-content, S) Nahal Pratzim & the Flour Cave — salt canyon hiking at Mount Sodom (/nahal-pratzim-guide) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep "nahal.pratzim\|pratzim\|flour.cave\|mount.sodom.*hike\|mt.sodom.*hike" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO matches. Mount Sodom (Har Sedom in Hebrew) is a 225m ridge on the southwest shore of the Dead Sea, composed almost entirely of halite (rock salt) and softer sedimentary rock — one of the world's only large-scale salt diapir landforms accessible to hikers. Nahal Pratzim is a narrow canyon carved by water erosion through this salt rock over 18,000–20,000 years; the canyon walls glow white/cream. The Flour Cave (Mearath HaKemah) is a chamber within the mountain where fine white salt and calcium sulfate dust coat the walls and floor, creating an otherworldly pure-white interior. Location: Route 90 km 193–194, near Neve Zohar, 20 km south of the main Dead Sea resort area — adds 20 min to a Dead Sea day trip. Three tiers: (1) Family-accessible — Flour Cave entrance only (15-min return; flat floor; eerie all-white chamber; no climbing required; headtorch needed); (2) Moderate — Nahal Pratzim and Sodom Circuit (AllTrails: 0.9 mi loop; 1–2 hrs; narrow slot canyon section requires turning sideways; Lot's Wife salt pillar viewpoint; panoramic views across the Dead Sea to the Jordanian plateau); (3) Hard — Lot's Wife + Amiaz Plateau full day hike (AllTrails: 12.3 miles, 1,597 ft elevation gain, 5.5–6 hrs; broad Sodom ridge traverse; exposed limestone plateau; exceptional Dead Sea panoramas; requires good fitness and navigation). Logistics: free access (no INPA entrance fee); Route 90 pull-off; no facilities; self-navigate (download AllTrails GPS offline); nearest services at Neve Zohar (Ein Bokek area, 5 km north). HONESTY: Flour Cave requires headtorch or phone torch — must state clearly; flash flood risk in the canyon and on Sodom ridge (same as all Negev wadis — check ims.gov.il); the "Lot's Wife" salt pillar is a natural formation carrying the traditional name — frame as geological feature + biblical tradition, not claimed archaeological fact; the canyon may be wet or muddy in winter (December–February) — some passages impassable after rain. Affiliate: DiscoverCars car hire CTA (Route 90 requires private vehicle; no public transport stop at trail), Booking.com Dead Sea / Neve Zohar accommodation CTA. Cross-links: dead-sea-guide.md (add Nahal Pratzim as half-day add-on), masada-dead-sea-day-trip.md (Nahal Pratzim is 30 km south of Masada — could form a full-day Dead Sea south itinerary), dead-sea-tours-compared.md (add "south Dead Sea" variant), negev-desert-hiking.md (add Sodom ridge as hard-day alternative). SEO: "Nahal Pratzim hike guide", "Flour Cave Israel Dead Sea", "Mount Sodom hike Israel", "salt canyon Israel", "Dead Sea hidden hike". Sources: alltrails.com/trail/israel/southern-district-hadarom/amiaz-plateau-nahal-pratzim-and-mearat-hakemah, israelandyou.com/nachal-pratzim, trover.com mount-sodom, goisrael.com. [iter370 research] status: ready

- [SHIPPED iter371] Jerusalem Pilgrimage Road (/jerusalem-pilgrimage-road) — SHA 5279b75 — CONFIRMED GAP: grep "pilgrimage.road\|siloam.*road\|pool.of.siloam\|siloam.*pool" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO matches. The city-of-david-jerusalem.md guide (SHIPPED iter337) covers Hezekiah's Tunnel and the Bronze Age City of David — this is a DISTINCT attraction. The Jerusalem Pilgrimage Road (also called the Stepped Street) is a 600-metre ancient paved street following the original Second Temple-period pilgrimage route from the Pool of Siloam (where worshippers immersed in a mikveh before ascending) up through the Jerusalem valley to the Temple Mount plaza. Excavation took 13 years (2012–2025) by the Israel Antiquities Authority under the City of David National Park. The road was ceremonially inaugurated on 15 September 2025 in a ceremony attended by PM Netanyahu, US Secretary of State Rubio, and US Ambassador Huckabee, and opened to public visitors in January 2026. PHYSICAL: the street is 600m long, 7.5–8m wide, paved with limestone slabs; over 100 column drums and architectural fragments found; a drainage channel runs below (also walkable); merchants' shops (tabernae) line both sides with archaeologically recovered bronze weights, coins, pottery. The Pool of Siloam at the south end (the start of the pilgrimage ascent) is a rock-cut pool first mentioned in the Gospel of John (9:7); the pool can hold thousands of worshippers; admission to the tunnel + pool complex is via City of David. DISTINCT from Hezekiah's Tunnel: the tunnel (SHIPPED in city-of-david-jerusalem.md) was a water conduit used in 701 BCE; the Pilgrimage Road was used in the 1st century CE as the main processional route to the Temple. DISTINCT from the Jewish Quarter / Cardo: the Pilgrimage Road is in the valley below the Old City walls, accessed through the City of David visitor complex, not from the Jewish Quarter. HONESTY: the site is in the Silwan neighbourhood, under Israeli administration as part of the City of David National Park — some visitors may encounter political context (the site's political symbolism was part of the inauguration ceremony); frame factually without taking sides; "Second Temple-period" is the archaeological designation (approximately 20 BCE–70 CE); the inauguration ceremony's political significance should be noted without editorialising; access requires coordination with City of David (cityofdavid.org.il) for ticketing (the Pilgrimage Road is a new addition to the City of David complex tour, likely requiring add-on ticket); hours and ticket prices should always be verified at cityofdavid.org.il before publication. Affiliate: GYG "City of David + Hezekiah's Tunnel" tour CTA (most tours likely being extended to include the road by publication time), Booking.com Jerusalem accommodation CTA. Cross-links: city-of-david-jerusalem.md (add Pilgrimage Road as major new 2026 section with guide link), christian-pilgrimage-holy-land.md (add Pilgrimage Road as new holy site attraction), jerusalem-old-city-walking-tour.md (update to include Pilgrimage Road). SEO: "Jerusalem Pilgrimage Road guide", "Pool of Siloam visitor guide", "ancient stepped street Jerusalem", "Second Temple pilgrimage route Israel", "City of David Pilgrimage Road". Sources: jpost.com/archaeology/article-885516, timesofisrael.com (2000-year-old Pilgrimage Road opens Jan 2026), cityofdavid.org.il, haaretz.com/archaeology (Pilgrimage Road coverage). [iter370 research] status: ready

- [P2] (seo-content, S) Via ferrata in Israel — Ramim Cliff vs Ravid routes comparison guide (/via-ferrata-israel) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep "ramim.*cliff\|ramim-cliff\|ravid.*ferrata\|ferrata.*ravid\|kibbutz.menara.*ferrata" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO matches (two backlog mentions of "via ferrata" are: SkyField Extreme Park Jaffa, and the Mount Arbel cliff-path note "not via-ferrata level" — neither is a standalone via ferrata comparison guide). Israel has two established via ferrata routes operated by dedicated adventure companies, neither of which is documented on any major English travel publisher. Route 1 — Ramim Cliff (King's Road Via Ferrata), Kibbutz Menara, Upper Galilee: Israel's first via ferrata, established 2017 by Ashdot Extreme; Level B/C (lower of the two); 1.2 km loop; the route traverses the Ramim Ridge above the Hula Valley with panoramic views over the Lebanon border hills, the Hula Lake Nature Reserve, and the northern Golan Heights; fixed iron rungs, cables, and ladder sections; total climbing time 2–3 hours including zip line descent; Ashdot Extreme provides all harness, helmet, and gloves equipment; suitable for visitors with basic fitness but no prior climbing experience; minimum age 12; group tours run daily (English guides available on request). Route 2 — Via Ferrata Ravid, Kibbutz Ravid, Lower Galilee: Israel's longest via ferrata as of 2026, operated by Israel Extreme / Adventure Israel; Level C (more technical than Ramim); approximately 900m linear route with multiple zip line sections and suspension bridges; the route traverses the cliffs of Har Ravid above the Sea of Galilee (Kinneret) with views of the lake, Tiberias, and the Golan Heights plateau; duration 3–4 hours; same harness/helmet/gloves package provided; suitable for visitors with basic fitness and no fear of heights but no prior climbing experience required; minimum age 14. Content: head-to-head comparison table (location, level, views, duration, price, minimum age, what's included); regional context (Upper Galilee vs Lower Galilee — combine with Rosh Hanikra+Banias day trip for Ramim; combine with Tiberias+Sea of Galilee day trip for Ravid); add-on: City of David Zipline (urban counterpart — 20-metre zip line with views of the Silwan valley from within the City of David archaeological complex; accessible from Jerusalem; different audience). HONESTY: all activities are harnessed and instructor-supervised — do not overstate danger; "Level C" is an Israeli operator grading (not the international UIAA ferrata scale) — clarify; prices should be verified on ashdotextreme.co.il and adventure-israel.com at time of publication; the routes are in the north (Upper and Lower Galilee) — access by private car only; pre-booking required. Affiliate: GetYourGuide Galilee adventure day trip CTA, DiscoverCars car hire CTA (both sites require car access from Haifa or Tiberias). Cross-links: hiking-in-israel.md (add via ferrata section), northern-israel-road-trip.md (when built — add Ramim as optional adventure segment), tiberias-guide.md (add Ravid as active day option), adventure-sports.md. SEO: "via ferrata Israel guide", "Ramim Cliff via ferrata", "Israel adventure hiking", "Kibbutz Menara via ferrata", "adventure climbing Israel". Sources: ashdotextreme.co.il/en/ramim-cliff-breathtaking-via-ferrata-israel-most-awesome-adventure, adventure-israel.com/home/via-ferrata-in-israel, israelextreme.tours/products/via-ferrata-ravid, hike-israel.com/israelextreme/viaferrata. [iter370 research] status: ready

- [P3] (seo-content, S) Knesset Museum Jerusalem — where Israeli democracy was born (/knesset-museum-jerusalem) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep "knesset.museum\|knesset-museum" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO matches. The Knesset Museum opened in late 2025 in the Froumine House (also known as "the first Knesset building") at 13 King George Street, Jerusalem — the building that served as Israel's parliament from 1950 to 1966 before the Knesset moved to its current building on Givat Ram. The museum tells the story of Israel's founding democracy: the declaration of independence, the first Knesset session in Jerusalem, the debates over Israel's constitutional foundation, key legislative moments including the Law of Return and the Basic Laws, and the personalities who shaped Israel's political culture. Location: King George Street in central Jerusalem — walkable from Mahane Yehuda market, Ben Yehuda pedestrian mall, Independence Park, and the Italian Synagogue. The building's physical architecture is significant: it retains its 1950s character and the original plenum chamber where foundational debates occurred. HONESTY: the museum opened relatively recently and hours/entry details may still be settling — always verify at the official site (knesset.gov.il/museum or similar); the Froumine House is separate from the current Knesset building in Givat Ram (which also offers public tours — a DISTINCT option); framing the first Knesset site as "where Israeli democracy was born" is an accurate editorial frame given the building's role in the foundational 1950–1966 period. Affiliate: GYG Jerusalem walking tour CTA (easily combined with a central Jerusalem walking tour), Booking.com Jerusalem accommodation CTA. Cross-links: jerusalem-old-city-walking-tour.md (add Knesset Museum as King George Street stop), israel-independence-guide.md (when built — Independence Day itinerary), mahane-yehuda-market-guide.md (add as cultural stop within walking distance). SEO: "Knesset Museum Jerusalem guide", "first Knesset building visit", "Israeli democracy museum", "Froumine House Jerusalem", "Jerusalem new museums 2026". Sources: jpost.com/israel-news/culture/article-863840 (Knesset Museum opening), knesset.gov.il, touristisrael.com/israel-travel-2026/80636. [iter370 research] status: ready

- [P2] (seo-content+monetization, M) New luxury hotels opening in Israel 2026 — ultimate guide to the year's biggest openings (/new-luxury-hotels-israel-2026) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep "new-hotel.*2026\|best-new-hotel\|hotel.*opening.*roundup\|nobu.*tel.aviv\|six.senses.*tel.aviv" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO matches (existing hotel guides are per-city: best-hotels-tel-aviv SHIPPED, best-hotels-jerusalem SHIPPED, best-hotels-eilat SHIPPED, best-hotels-dead-sea likely exists — none covers the 2026 openings roundup angle). TIMELY HOOK: Israeli tourism industry reports 17 major new hotel openings in 2026, representing the largest single-year expansion of Israeli hotel stock since pre-2000. Key properties for this roundup: (1) Six Senses Tel Aviv (opening 2026; Golden Triangle, Rothschild-Herzl-Lilienblum; Israel's most anticipated luxury opening; 140 rooms in 5 heritage buildings from 1909 Ahuzat Bayit settlement + new 42-story LEED Platinum tower; 10-floor spa with Holistic Anti-Aging Center; sky pool; chef-led restaurant; the largest historic preservation project in Tel Aviv history); (2) Nobu Hotel Tel Aviv (2026; Jaffa area; brand extension of the globally recognised Nobu restaurant group; 200+ rooms; signature Nobu sushi at the hotel restaurant; targets affluent international travellers who travel by brand recognition); (3) Isrotel Kayma Dead Sea (new eco-luxury concept; the Kayma brand targets sustainability-conscious luxury travellers; Dead Sea mineral pool; positioned as greener alternative to classic Dead Sea resort hotels); (4) Ein Hod Boutique Wellness Hotel (Ein Hod artist village, Carmel Coast; niche cultural-wellness property in Israel's most distinctive artists' colony; yoga, pottery, gallery access, Carmel forest; category of one); (5) Herzl Hotel Beersheba (new heritage hotel in the historic Beersheba Governor's House; ties into the Beersheba UNESCO nomination for the Ottoman-era Beer Sheba; appeals to heritage architecture travellers); (6) VERT Hotel Mitzpe Ramon — crater rim, 260 rooms by Africa Israel Hotels (planned; on track for 2027–2028 delivery — note as "coming soon"). Content structure: roundup table by category (design-led, wellness, eco, heritage, boutique) → per-property 150w editorial blurb → practical booking links → "who it's for" verdict. HONESTY: opening dates are subject to change — note publication date and advise readers to check directly; do not present "planned" hotels (VERT, some Nobu timelines) as open; use "expected" language; Six Senses Tel Aviv opening has slipped before (was late 2025, pushed to 2026) — verify status at time of publication; prices for newly opened properties change rapidly — link Booking.com rather than quoting rates. Monetization: Booking.com affiliate CTA for each confirmed-open property; Booking.com "new hotels" search CTA in intro; secondary GYG luxury Tel Aviv tour CTA. Cross-links: best-hotels-tel-aviv.md (SHIPPED — add cross-link to "2026 new openings" guide from intro section), israel-accommodation-guide.md (add roundup mention), tel-aviv-luxury-guide.md (when built). ## iter375 additions (2026-07-08 RESEARCH)

- [SHIPPED iter376 · SHA 8a36764] (seo-content, S) Tabgha Church Guide (/tabgha-church-guide) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep "tabgha" in BACKLOG = ZERO hits; grep "tabgha" in src/content/guides = only mentioned inside galilee-christian-sites-circuit.md, no standalone guide. Tabgha is the northwest Sea of Galilee site (5 km from Capernaum) containing two of the most-visited Christian churches in Israel: (1) Church of the Multiplication of Loaves and Fishes (Benedictine; 5th-century mosaic floor — the famous loaves-and-fish tile; ongoing benedictine monastery; cool stone interior); (2) Church of the Primacy of St. Peter / Mensa Christi (Franciscan; lakeside site of Jesus's post-Resurrection breakfast; natural rock "Mensa Christi" table). Multiple competitor standalone guides confirmed: israel-taxi.com/tabgha, touristisrael.com/tabgha, seetheholyland.net/tabgha, beinharimtours.com/tabgha-churches, israel-in-photos.com/tabgha. GYG and Viator both sell Sea of Galilee Christian half-day tour SKUs that stop at Tabgha as anchor site. WHY VALUABLE: Tabgha is a primary stop on ALL Galilee pilgrimage itineraries; high search intent from Christian visitors; standalone guide fills the gap between galilee-christian-sites-circuit (circuit format) and capernaum-visitor-guide (separate attraction, ~500m away). HONESTY: do not fabricate visitor counts or mass-celebration times; Benedictine monastery is an active religious community — note access restrictions during prayer hours. International travelling light festival launched its Israel edition at Yarkon Park (Ganei Yehoshua tropical garden, Tel Aviv) in November 2025 for a Nov–Jan seasonal run; the 2025/2026 edition ran Nov 13, 2025 – Jan 15, 2026. 300+ illuminated sculptures (many custom-created for Tel Aviv), 1km walking route through the subtropical garden, interactive installations, light + sound shows, European food market on-site; Israeli + Austrian artist collaboration. CONTENT: visitor experience (1km illuminated route after dark through subtropical Yarkon Park; giant sculpted animals + abstract forms up to 5m tall; timed light shows synced to music; selfie/photo stations; European food market with hot drinks and seasonal foods); practical info (Yarkon Park entrance near HaYarkon Park gates; accessible by bus, bike, taxi from central TLV; ticket required — ~₪80–120/person, always confirm at lumagica.com/en/israel-en; evening sessions from ~17:00 onward; 1.5–2h typical visit; dress warmly — December TLV evenings 10–15°C); photography (smartphone Night Mode works well; designed for Instagram sharing; arrive 30 min before session to avoid entry queues at peak weekend evenings). HONESTY: Lumagica runs November–January only — confirm annual dates at lumagica.com/en/israel-en (next edition expected Nov 2026); entry prices change year to year — never hardcode; "300+ sculptures" from 2025/2026 edition — future editions may vary; not guaranteed to return annually — confirm current edition before publishing. - [P2] (seo-content+monetization, M) Israel military history tourism guide (/israel-military-history-guide) — CONFIRMED GAP: zero P-tagged backlog entry and zero guide file for military history tourism hub; zero coverage on Lonely Planet, Timeout Israel, or any major travel brand. Major segment: diaspora Jewish visitors, military history enthusiasts, Israeli Defense Forces history buffs. Sites: Latrun (IDF Armored Corps Museum — 200+ tanks/armored vehicles on the hillside, world's 3rd largest collection; Route 1 Jerusalem–TLV highway; free + no booking; dramatic hilltop setting; ₪45 museum entry); Palmach Museum TLV (immersive walk-through museum of the pre-state underground fighters; highly produced exhibitions; advance booking required; ₪50; TLV HaYarkon Park area); Haganah Museum TLV (Allenby St; small but significant; pre-state defence organisation; ₪20); Ammunition Hill Jerusalem (Six-Day War 1967 battle site; the Paratroopers' Museum; last major defensive position before Western Wall liberation; free + small parking fee; east Jerusalem near Ramat Eshkol); Air Force Museum Hatzerim (IAF Museum near Beersheba; 250 aircraft including original IAF Spitfire, Mystère, Phantom, F-15, Kfir; world-class open-air + indoor collection; free; requires car — no public transport; 45 min from Beersheba, 90 min from TLV); Etzel Museum TLV (Irgun/pre-state paramilitary; Small museum on TLV beachfront near Hilton; free; fringe interest). HONESTY: frame as historical/educational tourism, not celebration of violence; individual battles framed with Israeli and (where documented) opposing side losses; no fabricated casualty numbers — use documented figures only; Ammunition Hill is in East Jerusalem — logistics note needed. Affiliate: GYG private military history tour (confirmed product exists on GYG for TLV Haganah+Palmach); discovercars.com car rental for Latrun + Hatzerim (not accessible by public transit). Dense cross-links: jewish-heritage-israel, yad-vashem-visitor-guide, israel-independence-day, israel-5-vs-7-vs-10-days, first-time-in-israel. SEO: "Israel military history tour", "Latrun tank museum guide", "Palmach Museum Tel Aviv", "Ammunition Hill Jerusalem guide", "IAF Museum Hatzerim Israel". Sources: iter385 Agent 1 + Agent 2 research; IDF Armored Corps Museum site; latrun.co.il; iaf.org.il (IAF Museum). [iter385 research] status: ready

- [P2] (seo-content+monetization, S) Israel in autumn travel guide (/israel-in-autumn) — CONFIRMED GAP: `israel-in-summer.md` exists; `christmas-in-israel.md` covers Dec winter; `best-time-to-visit-israel.md` covers seasonality in one paragraph; but NO dedicated autumn/October guide. October–November is Israel's PEAK tourist season: 23–27°C, no humidity of summer, no winter rain, Sukkot holiday atmosphere (outdoor sukkahs everywhere, Jerusalem at its most festive), Hula Valley crane migration (Agamon Hula birdwatching — 200,000+ cranes on stopover; one of world's great wildlife spectacles; Sukkot/October window), wine harvest at Golan Heights wineries (September–October; "vendange" tours possible), Dead Sea salt formations (autumn light = ideal for photography), national park visits (cooler than summer = better hiking; trails uncrowded post-Sukkot). Advance booking advisory: Sukkot week = hotels sold out 3–4 months ahead; prices 200–300% surge during Sukkot; book immediately after Passover for October travel. Key experiences: Sukkot in Jerusalem (WOW atmosphere; birkat kohanim at Western Wall), Hula Valley birdwatching (cross-link birdwatching-israel when built), harvest at Golan winery, Ein Gedi autumn hike (lower water = easier trail access), Tabgha + Galilee in October light, Eilat in October (sea temperature still 26°C, shoulder season prices). HONESTY: Sukkot dates shift annually per Hebrew calendar — always note exact 2026/2027 dates; "off-peak" in late November genuinely is quieter; October = peak, not shoulder. Affiliate: Booking.com October Israel hotels CTA (advance booking hook) + GYG Sukkot Jerusalem tour CTA + Viator Hula Valley birdwatching tour CTA. SEO: "Israel in autumn", "Israel in October", "Israel October travel guide 2026", "best time to visit Israel October". Sources: Agent 1 + Agent 2 + site seasonal content audit; best-time-to-visit-israel.md; sukkot-in-israel backlog. [iter385 research] status: ready

- [P2] (seo-content+monetization, M) Golan Heights wine route self-drive guide (/golan-heights-wine-route) — CONFIRMED GAP: `israel-wine-wineries.md` is a national wine overview (Galilee + Golan + Judean Hills + Negev); `golan-heights-guide.md` mentions wineries in 1-2 paragraphs; but NO self-drive circuit guide exists for the Golan wine route itself. DISTINCT from national wine hub (this = self-drive circuit, not overview). Content: self-drive circuit from Tiberias (2h base) or Kiryat Shmona (1h base); Golan Heights Winery at Katzrin (largest in Israel, 5.5M bottles/year; Yarden + Gamla labels; full visitor centre; tasting ₪50–90; open Sunday–Friday 9am–5pm; no Shabbat), Galil Mountain Winery near Kibbutz Yiron (560m elevation; outstanding Barbera and Yiron flagship; boutique tasting experience; book ahead), Pelter Winery (Upper Galilee; award-winning boutique; small production; reservation required), Assaf Winery (family boutique; Lower Golan; visit by appointment), Druze village lunch stop (Majdal Shams apple orchards + fresh village lunch at local restaurant; Mas'ade or Ein Qiniyye). Drive sequence: Katzrin (Golan Heights Winery) → Majdal Shams lunch → return via Banias/Nimrod Fortress option for non-drinkers; OR Kiryat Shmona base → Galil Mountain Winery → Pelter → lunch village → Katzrin. Seasonal note: harvest season September–October = busiest and most atmospheric; must call ahead for boutique wineries year-round; Shabbat = all wineries closed. HONESTY: Golan Heights international status — same neutral framing as golan-heights-guide.md; winery prices = ranges only; harvest dates vary; never claim specific wine awards without linking verified sources. Affiliate: Viator Golan wine tour CTA (group tours with transport — essential as you'll be drinking) + discovercars.com car rental CTA for self-drivers who designate a non-drinking driver. Dense cross-links: golan-heights-guide, israel-wine-wineries, golan-druze-villages, banias-guide, nimrod-fortress-guide, northern-israel-road-trip (when built). - [P2] (seo-content+monetization, M) Israel comprehensive diving hub (/israel-diving-guide) — CONFIRMED GAP: `eilat-diving-snorkeling.md` covers Eilat specifically; `yam-caesarea-guide.md` covers Caesarea marine NP; but NO hub covers ALL Israel dive sites. Competitor gap: no major English travel brand has a comprehensive Israel diving guide covering both Red Sea + Mediterranean. Sites: (1) Eilat Red Sea — coral gardens at Coral Beach Reserve (INPA; entry fee; snorkel/dive permitted; protected corals; ~25m visibility), Satil missile boat wreck (IAF sank the Egyptian missile boat during 1973 war; now major wreck dive; 25m; advanced), Moses Rock (soft corals + Napoleon fish; beginner-friendly), Japanese Gardens (fish diversity; suitable for all levels; closest to beach), The Caves (cavern dive; intermediate); dive operators: Manta Diving + Deep Six Eilat + Aqua Sport Eilat (established operators with PADI certification); (2) Caesarea Mediterranean — Underwater archaeology park at Yam Caesarea Marine NP (INPA diving trails through Herodian harbour ruins from 20 BCE; coral reef communities + seagrass; dive with Old Caesarea Diving Center — sole INPA-permitted operator; seasonal access October–June; visibility 10–15m; archaeological framing = unique dive experience globally), standard Mediterranean diving at Rosh Hanikra chalk formations (northern coast); (3) Achziv National Park Mediterranean (sea caves, rocky shore, Mediterranean species; day-entry required; snorkel-only for most areas); (4) PADI certification landscape in Israel (multiple operators in Eilat; 3-day open water course available; Eilat = ideal certification environment — warm water + good visibility year-round). HONESTY: Caesarea archaeological dive requires advance booking with sole authorized operator; diving in Eilat requires PADI card or booking through operator intro dive; Red Sea visibility/conditions subject to seasonal haboob (dust storms); NEVER claim "best diving in the world" comparisons — frame as distinctive experiences. Affiliate: GYG/Viator Eilat dive experience CTAs + PADI/SSI certification course CTAs. Dense cross-links: eilat-diving-snorkeling, yam-caesarea-guide, eilat-travel-guide, caesarea-guide. - [P2] (seo-content+monetization, M) Israel + Greece multi-destination itinerary (/israel-greece-itinerary) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "greece.*israel.*itinerary|israel.*greece.*itinerary|/israel-greece|/israel-and-greece|\[P[23]\].*greece" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO P-item matches — "greece" appears only as a word in competitor site lists and Hellenistic historical context, never as a standalone trip combination topic. Multi-country Mediterranean travel growing: El Al, EasyJet, Ryanair all fly TLV→ATH in ~3h (fares ₪200–600). "Greece Israel itinerary" and "Israel and Greece vacation" are genuine search queries from European + American travelers planning dual Mediterranean countries. No Israel travel site has this combination guide. Israel and Greece share: Hellenistic period history (Alexander's conquest of Canaan 332 BCE; Septuagint translation; Herodian architecture Greek influence; Caesarea's amphitheater + hippodrome modeled on Rome-via-Greece), Mediterranean climate, wine culture, beaches. Content: 14-day sample itinerary (6 days Israel → fly ATH → 7 days Greek islands); which Greek islands pair best thematically (Crete for history + archaeology; Rhodes for Crusader heritage + beaches; Santorini/Mykonos for romance; Corfu for lush greenery); split strategy (fly into TLV, exit via ATH, or reverse); entry requirements (both visa-free for most nationalities; no double-entry issues); Israeli passport holders: Greece is Schengen, no issue; logistics (TLV→ATH Arkia + El Al + EasyJet + Ryanair; within-Greece: fly-ferry combo from Athens to islands); sample 14-day split (Days 1-6: Jerusalem + Dead Sea + Tel Aviv; Day 7: fly TLV→ATH 09:00 arr. 11:00; Days 8-14: Athens 1d + island 5d); booking strategy (Israel hotels book earlier than Greek islands — reverse order from cheapest-to-book; flight ticket recommended 6–10 weeks out for good fares). HONESTY: Greece has far more islands and a much larger tourism infrastructure than Israel — set expectation that Israel component = smaller geographic footprint, higher cultural density; Haifa ferry to Greece is NOT a viable regular transport option (sporadic; frame as background note only); Greek islands accommodation in summer (July–August) must be booked months in advance. Affiliate: Skyscanner flight CTA for TLV→ATH (primary — high booking value), Booking.com both countries, GYG tours in both Israel and Athens. Cross-links: first-time-in-israel, israel-5-vs-7-vs-10-days, israel-jordan-itinerary (sibling multi-country guide), tel-aviv-guide, best-time-to-visit-israel (seasonal alignment), ben-gurion-airport-guide. Sources: iter390 research — El Al TLV-ATH schedule; roughguides.com/destinations/europe/greece; lonely planet Mediterranean cruise research. [iter390 research] status: ready

- [SHIPPED iter391] Israel vs Turkey: which should you visit comparison guide (/israel-vs-turkey) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "/israel-vs-turkey|/turkey-vs-israel|israel.*versus.*turkey|turkey.*versus.*israel|\[P[23]\].*israel.*vs.*turkey" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO matches — "turkey" appears only in "Israeli street food guide" (falafel vs shawarma vs turkey-spit context), never as a travel comparison. "Israel vs Turkey" is a genuine high-intent travel query: both destinations offer beach holidays + ancient history + Mediterranean food culture + accessible from Europe at comparable price points. European travelers (UK, Germany, France) regularly compare both when planning Middle East / Eastern Mediterranean holidays. No Israel travel site has this page. Turkey typically wins on scale + beach variety; Israel wins on cultural intensity + religious significance + safe urban environment + unique desert. Content: head-to-head comparison matrix (8 criteria rows × 2 columns): cost of travel (Israel ₪600–900/day mid-range; Turkey TRY equivalent similar but weakening lira fluctuations = uncertainty), safety (Israel = highly secure + trained security; Turkey = safe for tourists in main cities, avoid southeast), visa requirements (both visa-free for EU/US/UK — Turkey e-Visa $50, Israel ETA-IL ~$7), beaches (Turkey wins on variety + scale — Mediterranean, Aegean, Black Sea; Israel = 273km coastline, concentrated Eilat + Mediterranean coast), history (Israel wins on sheer historical density per square km; Turkey has Istanbul Byzantine + Ephesus + Cappadocia), food (both excellent; Israel more dietary-restriction friendly; Turkey more price-competitive), nightlife (Israel TLV = world-class; Istanbul + Bodrum = strong also), best for (families: Turkey Bodrum/Side; religious tourism: Israel; history buffs: both; beach holiday: Turkey for variety; unique cultural depth: Israel). Verdict section: who Israel is for, who Turkey is for. HONESTY: never claim one is "better" overall — both have distinct strengths; political comparison must be strictly avoided (Turkey-Israel diplomatic relations have been complex since 2010; guide is travel comparison only); Turkey price depends heavily on exchange rate — use ranges + note volatility. Affiliate: Booking.com both countries CTA (high conversion for comparison pages), Skyscanner multi-destination flights, GYG tour CTAs for both. Cross-links: first-time-in-israel, israel-cost-budget, israel-in-summer, best-beaches-israel. NONE addresses the PLANNING stage: "I'm looking at Mediterranean cruises — should I choose one that includes Israel?" This is a different funnel position with high commercial value (cruise booking average = $1,500–$3,000/person). Major cruise lines that include Israel: MSC Cruises (Mediterranean routes from Genoa/Civitavecchia/Barcelona include Haifa or Ashdod; seasonal); Royal Caribbean (select Mediterranean sailings; typically Ashdod); Costa Cruises (Italian-market Mediterranean routes); Norwegian Cruise Line (select itineraries). Port comparison (the core decision): Haifa port (Mediterranean coast, excellent for Galilee + Nazareth + Sea of Galilee circuit; 2–3h to these attractions; Israel's most scenic port city itself; Bahá'í Gardens free entry); Ashdod port (south of Tel Aviv, 45-min drive to Jerusalem = the BEST Israel port for Jerusalem day-trip; also easy Dead Sea access; less scenic port city itself); Jerusalem from Haifa = 3.5h drive each way — barely achievable in a typical 8h port day (DON'T mislead readers on this). Content: which cruise lines offer Israel stops; Haifa vs Ashdod comparison table; sample best port-day use per port; ship excursion vs independent tour (GYG/private driver); Shabbat issue (Friday port arrivals = very limited independent touring options — flag strongly); extending cruise with pre/post hotel stay in Israel; whether Israel port is worth it (editorial verdict: YES for Ashdod → Jerusalem; Haifa → Galilee also excellent); what Israel adds to a Mediterranean cruise vs a straight Greece/Italy/Spain itinerary. HONESTY: Haifa to Jerusalem in a cruise port day is extremely tight — must clearly state this; never imply ship excursions are better or worse without specific comparison; Israel port schedules are seasonal and can change year-to-year — link specific cruise lines rather than hard-code routes. Affiliate: GYG private driver tour CTAs from both Haifa and Ashdod (high-value product), Booking.com Israel hotel CTAs for pre/post-cruise nights. Cross-links: haifa-cruise-port-excursions (when built), cruise-shore-excursions-israel (SHIPPED), haifa-travel-guide, jerusalem-old-city-walking-tour, ben-gurion-airport-guide. SEO: "Mediterranean cruise Israel stop", "cruise to Israel guide", "is Israel worth it on a cruise", "Haifa vs Ashdod cruise port", "should I book cruise with Israel". Sources: iter390 research — msccruises.com/en-us (Mediterranean itineraries), royalcaribbean.com/cruises-to/israel, cruisecritic.com/ports/israel, beinharimtours.com/cruise-shore-excursions. [iter390 research] status: ready

- [P3] (monetization+seo-content, S) Israel Airbnb vs hotel: accommodation decision guide (/israel-airbnb-vs-hotel) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "airbnb.*vs.*hotel.*israel|/israel-airbnb-vs-hotel|\[P[23]\].*airbnb.*vs.*hotel" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO matches. EXISTING COVERAGE: accommodation-guide.md (SHIPPED) covers types of accommodation broadly; kibbutz-hotels-guide.md (SHIPPED iter261) covers kibbutz hotels specifically; best-hotels-tel-aviv/jerusalem/eilat/dead-sea are distinct per-city guides. NONE addresses the binary "Airbnb or hotel?" decision that travelers Google constantly ("is Airbnb good in Israel?", "should I use Airbnb in Israel?"). This is a monetization page: recommendation for Booking.com (hotels) vs general Airbnb browsing, with affiliate CTAs for both. Israel-specific Airbnb landscape varies dramatically by city: Tel Aviv (massive Airbnb supply — 5,000+ listings; entire apartments in Florentine, Neve Tzedek, Rothschild — excellent for stays 3+ nights and self-cooking); Jerusalem (supply limited — Jewish Quarter properties rare due to religious sensitivities; Muslim Quarter = small supply; best areas are German Colony, Baka; many older properties without elevator — accessibility issue); Eilat (mostly hotels — small Airbnb supply; hotels offer beach access + Dead Sea minerals amenity; Airbnb limited value here); Dead Sea (essentially 0 Airbnb supply — only hotel complexes have beach + mineral pool access; Airbnb stays would be 40+ min from the actual sea). Content: decision tree (how long? budget? cooking? neighborhood immersion?); city-by-city recommendation; pros/cons table; hidden cost comparison (Airbnb cleaning fees + service fee can erase apparent savings on short stays); Israel-specific Airbnb tips (host often speaks English; some listings are investment properties with automated check-in; Jewish holiday closures — some hosts take properties off-market during Passover/High Holidays; neighborhood context is valuable from hosts). HONESTY: Airbnb availability in Israel fluctuates by area + regulation; Israeli cities are compact — hotel location matters less than in sprawling cities; never claim one is universally cheaper without acknowledging cleaning fees. Affiliate: Booking.com CTA (primary — hotel booking with clear commission path), Airbnb referral link, Hostelworld for budget travelers. Cross-links: best-hotels-tel-aviv, accommodation-guide, tel-aviv-guide, jerusalem-travel-guide, eilat-hotels-guide. SEO: "Airbnb Israel", "is Airbnb good in Israel", "Airbnb vs hotel Tel Aviv", "best way to book accommodation Israel". Sources: iter390 research — Airbnb Israel listings audit; booking.com Israel hotels; tripadvisor Israel accommodation forum. [iter390 research] status: ready

- [P3] (seo-content, S) Israeli music culture and live venues guide (/israel-music-tourism) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "eurovision.*israel.*guide|\[P[23]\].*eurovision|/israel-music-culture|/israel-music-tourism|\[P[23]\].*music.*culture.*tourism" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO matches. EXISTING COVERAGE: israel-music-festivals.md (P2 backlog L140) = festivals calendar guide (Red Sea Jazz, Israel Festival, TLV Jazz, etc.); israel-events-festivals.md = events calendar. NEITHER covers the live music VENUE scene (specific clubs + venues to visit) or the cultural framing that helps music-curious travelers understand Israeli music's character. Israel's Eurovision 2024 win (Eden Golan, "Hurricane") generated massive global curiosity about Israeli pop music that has not been addressed editorially. Israeli music is genuinely diverse and distinctive: Israeli folk (HaYehudim, HaBanot Nechama, Mashina), mizrahi (Omer Adam, Sarit Hadad, Dudu Aharon), Israeli rock (Berry Sakharof, Hadag Nachash), jazz (Jerusalem Jazz scene), classical (Israel Philharmonic at Mann Auditorium), electronic (TLV underground — BarzilaI, Haoman 17). Content: brief introduction to Israeli music genres for traveler context; key venues (Barby Tel Aviv — capacity 1,000, Israel's most important rock venue, 3 Kibbutz Galuyot St, Tel Aviv, tickets via Leaan.co.il; Levontin 7 — intimate indie/alternative basement venue, 7 Levontin St, Tel Aviv; Yellow Submarine Jerusalem — weekly concerts Fri nights, multiple genres, Heleni HaMalka St, Jerusalem; Hamifal Haifa — converted industrial space, all-music genres, arts complex; Zappa Club TLV at Kfar Hamaccabiah, Ramat Gan); how to find concerts (TimeOut Israel events, Eventer.co.il, Leaan.co.il — Israeli ticketing platforms explained for foreign credit cards); Eurovision tourism angle (Eden Golan's Israeli pop career, where to find Israeli chart music, how to learn songs before visiting); Mann Auditorium + Israel Philharmonic (season tickets vs day-of; when IPO performs with international guests; building itself — Tel Aviv Brutalist landmark). HONESTY: Israeli music venues are not known internationally and require language navigation (Hebrew-only booking pages — note Leaan.co.il + Eventer.co.il have English versions or Google Translate works); never fabricate artists' tour schedules; Mann Auditorium / IPO season subject to programming changes. SEO: "Israeli music scene", "Tel Aviv music venues", "Israel music culture guide", "Israeli pop music", "where to see live music Tel Aviv". Sources: iter390 research — barby.co.il, levontin7.co.il, ipl.co.il (Israel Philharmonic), timeoutisrael.com/music, leaan.co.il. [iter390 research] status: ready

- [P3] (seo-content+monetization, S) Israel + Cyprus combination travel guide (/israel-cyprus-trip-guide) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "\[P[23]\].*cyprus.*israel|\[P[23]\].*israel.*cyprus|/israel-cyprus|/cyprus-from-israel|/israel-and-cyprus" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO P-item matches — "cyprus" appears only in the Jerusalem museums guide in a historical/archaeological context (Cyprus Aphrodite figurines in museum collection), never as a standalone trip combination topic. Cyprus is 45-minute flight from Tel Aviv (shortest flight from Israel to any EU/Schengen country): El Al, Wizz Air, Ryanair, EasyJet all operate TLV→LCA (Larnaca) and TLV→PFO (Paphos) daily. Israel-Cyprus combination is very popular with European travelers who want Mediterranean island + cultural Middle East experience in one trip; also attractive for Israelis wanting EU neighbor (large Israeli community in Limassol). Shared cultural threads: both countries have Bronze Age Canaanite connections (Cyprus = Alashiya in ancient texts, traded with Canaan extensively); Greek Orthodox Christianity present in both; wine culture (Cyprus has Commandaria, one of the world's oldest wines; pairs naturally with Israel wine tourism content); Mediterranean beaches. Cyprus adds to Israel trip: EU Schengen-adjacent environment (no Schengen yet but expected; current entry visa-free for most passports), Paphos Archaeological Park (UNESCO-listed; ancient theater, mosaics, Tombs of the Kings), Aphrodite Hills region, Troodos wine villages, Limassol Carnival (February), Akamas Peninsula hike (national park). Louis ferry Haifa→Limassol (seasonal service; approximately 8h overnight; check schedule at louisferries.com — NOT always operating). Sample 12-day itinerary: Days 1-7 Israel (Jerusalem 3d, Dead Sea, Tel Aviv); Day 8 fly TLV→LCA 09:00 arr. 10:00; Days 8-12 Cyprus (Paphos 2d, Limassol 2d, Nicosia day trip). HONESTY: Louis/Grimaldi Haifa-Limassol ferry schedule is NOT guaranteed — always verify at ferriesingreece.com or directferries.co.uk before writing; Cyprus is NOT Schengen (as of 2026) — do not claim it is; Limassol has a large Israeli expat community which makes it feel familiar — some travelers find this a plus, others a minus (frame neutrally); Cyprus has significantly less cultural intensity than Israel — honest set of expectations needed. Affiliate: Skyscanner TLV→LCA/PFO flights (primary), Booking.com Cyprus hotels, GYG tours in Cyprus + Israel. Cross-links: israel-greece-itinerary (when built; sibling multi-country guide), first-time-in-israel, haifa-travel-guide, ben-gurion-airport-guide, israel-wine-wineries (wine tourism connection). SEO: "Israel and Cyprus trip", "Cyprus from Israel guide", "Israel Cyprus itinerary 2026", "Tel Aviv to Cyprus holiday", "Cyprus Israel vacation". Sources: iter390 research — cyprusisland.net, visitcyprus.com, directferries.co.uk Haifa-Limassol. [iter390 research] status: ready

- [SHIPPED iter396 · SHA df92bb3] Museum of Tolerance Jerusalem guide (/museum-of-tolerance-jerusalem) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "museum.of.tolerance|\[P[23]\].*tolerance.jerusalem|/museum-of-tolerance" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO P-item matches. EXISTING COVERAGE: jewish-heritage-israel.md (shipped) covers Yad Vashem and Jewish heritage broadly; museum-of-tolerance-jerusalem has no mention in any guide or backlog item. COMPETITOR EVIDENCE: museumoftolerance.com/motj (official site); tripadvisor.com/Attraction_Review-museum_of_tolerance_jerusalem (recent reviews since 2024 opening); no Israel travel editorial site has a dedicated guide yet — genuinely new institution (completed 2023–2025 phased opening). Museum of Tolerance Jerusalem (MOTJ) is a 150,000 sq ft cultural center in the heart of Jerusalem (near Mamilla/Jaffa Gate), distinct from Yad Vashem (Holocaust memorial) and the Tolerance Museum Los Angeles (Simon Wiesenthal Center sister institution). Key features: "From Darkness to Light" exhibit with Oct 7 survivor testimonies (powerful, age-appropriate); children's museum wing (age 5+); 1,000-seat amphitheater for performances + events; permanent exhibits on coexistence, Jewish history, human rights; temporary exhibitions. Practical: admission ~$18–22 adult; advance booking recommended (museumoftolerance.com/motj/tickets); Shabbat closure (Sat); dress code (modest); accessible (wheelchair-friendly throughout). This is one of the highest-profile new Jerusalem attractions and will generate search traffic fast given its high-profile opening and media coverage. GYG Jerusalem cultural tour CTAs + Booking.com Jerusalem hotel CTAs. Cross-links: jerusalem-guide, jewish-heritage-israel, yad-vashem-guide (if exists, else jerusalem-guide), mamilla-district (if exists). SEO: "Museum of Tolerance Jerusalem", "MOTJ Jerusalem guide", "new Jerusalem museums 2025", "things to do Jerusalem cultural". HONESTY: never fabricate exhibit content details — use only confirmed official descriptions; Oct 7 exhibit is emotionally intense — include a note for sensitive travelers; admission prices change — phrase as approximate. [iter395 research] status: ready

(/israel-vs-egypt) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "vs\.egypt|egypt\.vs|\[P[23]\].*egypt.*comparison|/israel-vs-egypt" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO P-item matches — "egypt" appears only in border-crossing context and jordan-egypt combined tour mentions, never as a standalone comparison topic. Content: side-by-side 11-criteria table (cost per day, beach quality, history depth, food scene, safety, visa ease, climate, accommodation variety, LGBTQ+ context, solo female safety, value for money); decision matrix by traveller type (history first → Israel; beach + budget → Egypt Sinai resorts; pilgrimage → Israel; adventure + budget → Egypt); Israel unique advantages (Dead Sea float, Jerusalem Old City, Western Wall, living democracy); Egypt unique advantages (Pyramids + Sphinx, Luxor temple complex, Nile cruise, Aswan); combined trip angle (Eilat–Aqaba–Taba crossings exist; most travellers should choose one country per trip unless doing 3+ weeks); safety framing HONEST: Egypt Sinai has active FCO/State Dept security advisories — frame cautiously (Cairo + Luxor + Aswan generally safe for tourists; Sinai peninsula has travel-advisory warnings; link to current advisories, never fabricate safety ratings). Affiliate CTAs: GYG Israel tours CTA + Skyscanner flights to Israel from Europe + Booking.com Israel hotels. Wine tourism segment is growing: myisraelwinetours.com, winetraveler.com/israel rank for "Israel wine country road trip" with no major editorial guide. Affiliate CTAs: DiscoverCars (car hire — essential for wine trail) + Booking.com Rosh Pinna or Katzrin accommodation. - [P3] (seo-content, S) Karmiel Dance Festival guide (/karmiel-dance-festival-guide) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "karmiel|dance.*festival.*karmiel|\[P[23]\].*karmiel|/karmiel-dance" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO P-item matches. Israel's largest cultural festival (30+ years since 1987; 3-day event; 80+ shows; opening parade of 3,000 dancers in costume with orchestras; folk + modern + jazz + hip-hop + international troupes). 2026 dates: August 4–6. Annual event every August in Karmiel, Northern Galilee. Content: why go (Israel's answer to Edinburgh festival; free outdoor stages + ticketed indoor shows; international + Israeli troupes; community atmosphere; Northern Galilee backdrop); 2026 dates August 4–6 (HONESTY: dates shift slightly year to year — always link official karmiel-dance.co.il for current schedule, never hardcode future years); venue (Karmiel Amphitheatre + City Park + public squares); ticket types (outdoor main stage = free entry with pre-registration; indoor ticketed shows ₪80–₪150; special opening parade = free public event); getting there (no direct TLV–Karmiel bus: take to Haifa, then Egged bus 301 or 361 to Karmiel ~45 min; drive: Route 70 from Akko–Haifa–Tiberias; 1.5h from TLV); accommodation (Karmiel hotels limited; best to base in Haifa 40 min away or Tiberias 30 min away + commute; Haifa: Hotel Nof or boutique choices; book 3 months ahead for festival week); what to pack (comfortable shoes for outdoor stages; light clothing; August Galilee can reach 32°C but evenings cooler; bring portable seating/blanket for outdoor sessions). Affiliate CTAs: Booking.com Haifa hotels + GYG Galilee day trip CTA (for combining with festival visit). - [P3] (seo-content, S) Dead Sea Marathon spectator & runner guide (/dead-sea-marathon-guide) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "dead.sea.marathon|marathon.*dead.sea|\[P[23]\].*marathon|/dead-sea-marathon" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO P-item matches. World's lowest marathon (world record — start line at ~430m below sea level; genuinely unique bucket-list running experience). Annual event each February (2026: February 6th). COMPETITOR EVIDENCE: no Israel travel site has an editorial guide; deadsea-marathon.com (official) + running blogs (letsrun.com, runtheworld.com) cover it as a race review but no travel guide exists covering the experience holistically. Content: why it's unique (below-sea-level = higher atmospheric pressure = more oxygen density per breath → many runners report slightly better-than-expected times compared to altitude-impact altitude races; unique "bonus" framing; comparison with world's highest marathon — Everest Base Camp — as its mirror); distances available (full 42.195km, half marathon, 10km, 5km fun run → all registered via official site, international participants welcome); course description (flat first half along Dead Sea shoreline, slight climb on second half — honest: not flat throughout); logistics for international travellers (Masada + Dead Sea accommodation; travel from Jerusalem ~90 min by car; Tel Aviv ~2.5h; Dead Sea resort strip = Ein Bokek — marathon finish zone; accommodation book 4 months ahead for February race weekend); combining with sightseeing (post-race Dead Sea float — muscle recovery + unique experience; Masada sunrise on Saturday/Friday before race on Friday; En Gedi nature reserve day before race for shaded hiking); training notes (honest: Dead Sea's higher atmospheric pressure MAY support slightly faster pacing but effect is modest — do not overclaim; heat in July–August for training but Feb race is cool 15–20°C); registration (link official deadsea-marathon.com — never fabricate deadlines or pricing; international entry ~₪300–400 historically but verify). Affiliate CTAs: Booking.com Dead Sea hotels + GYG Masada tour CTA. Cross-links: dead-sea-guide, masada-dead-sea-day-trip, israel-adventure-sports, israel-national-parks-pass. SEO: "Dead Sea Marathon guide", "world's lowest marathon Israel", "Dead Sea Marathon travel tips", "Israel running event bucket list". Sources: deadsea-marathon.com, touristisrael.com (event listing), letsrun.com Dead Sea Marathon race reports, booking.com Dead Sea hotels. [iter400 research] status: ready

- [P3] (seo-content+monetization, S) Jerusalem secular nightlife guide (/jerusalem-nightlife-guide) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "jerusalem.nightlife|nightlife.*jerusalem|\[P[23]\].*jerusalem.*nightlife|/jerusalem-nightlife" against full BACKLOG.md returns ZERO P-item matches. EXISTING COVERAGE: tel-aviv-nightlife.md (SHIPPED) is specific to Tel Aviv; jerusalem-food-guide.md (SHIPPED) covers restaurants; israel-evening-activities.md (SHIPPED) covers cultural evening experiences. NO guide covers secular Jerusalem's bar/pub/live music nightlife scene — a genuine gap since Jerusalem has a lively secular scene that tourists routinely miss in favour of its religious identity. RATIONALE: secular Jerusalem nightlife centres on 3 districts: Mahane Yehuda market-to-bar transformation (market stalls →bars + cocktail pop-ups after 21:00); Russian Compound (Mifletzet bars, The Barrel, Sira — eclectic mix of cocktail bars and craft beer); Emek Refaim / German Colony (quieter, café-bar culture, Tmol Shilshom literary café-bar, Caffit restaurant into bar mode). Notable venues: Birman (classical music listening bar, unique); Stereo (jazz-influenced cocktail bar); HaKosem bar in market area; Lab club (electronic music underground). Content: realistic expectation-setting (Jerusalem's nightlife is quieter than TLV; Shabbat from Friday sundown means Friday nights are quiet in religious/central areas while secular bars near German Colony or Mahane Yehuda area are open — HONESTY: many bars in central Jerusalem close early Friday; Saturday is revival night as Shabbat ends after dark); Mahane Yehuda market transformation (07:00 fresh produce market → 21:00 bar scene; same stalls, different vibe; go after 21:30 for full atmosphere); Russian Compound district (10-min walk from Jaffa Gate; lower key, local scene; good for 22:00+ drinks); bar/club closing times (Jerusalem bars typically close 02:00–03:00, later on Saturday; nothing starts before 23:00 for clubs); transport back (shared sherut taxis from Mahane Yehuda area run late on weekends). Affiliate CTAs: Booking.com Jerusalem hotels (stay near German Colony for nightlife access) + GYG Jerusalem evening tour CTA. Cross-links: jerusalem-food-guide, israel-evening-activities, shabbat-guide, whats-open-on-shabbat, israel-events-festivals. SEO: "Jerusalem nightlife guide", "bars Jerusalem Israel", "Jerusalem after dark", "secular Jerusalem bars", "Mahane Yehuda bar scene". Sources: touristisrael.com/tel-aviv-nightlife (TLV comparison), timeout.co.il/jerusalem/bars, tripadvisor.com Jerusalem Nightlife experiences, jerusalem-post.com nightlife features. - [P2] (seo-content+monetization, M) Israel road trip with kids — family self-drive guide (/israel-road-trip-with-kids) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "road.trip.*kids|road.trip.*child|family.*road.trip|road-trip-kids|with-kids.*road-trip|family-road-trip" against full BACKLOG.md + DONE.md returns ZERO matches. NONE addresses the specific parental pain points for family self-drive trips: car seat rules, drive-time tolerances for children, kid-specific attraction filters, pool hotel priority, shade/cool stops in summer heat. COMPETITOR EVIDENCE: familiesfortravel.com.au/israel-with-kids-road-trip, thetickettotravel.com/israel-road-trip-with-kids, wanderingblonde.com/israel-with-kids all rank for "israel road trip with kids"; DiscoverCars and most rental companies offer child-seat supplements but no travel editorial covers Israeli car seat law. WHY VALUABLE: dual revenue hook (DiscoverCars car rental with child-seat supplement + Booking.com family-room hotels); family travel is a strong monetization audience; strong long-tail "israel road trip with children / toddler / baby". Content: Israeli car seat law (age/weight requirements — seat required for children under 135cm or 36kg; baby/toddler rearward-facing seat mandatory; DiscoverCars + Hertz/Eldan offer child-seat supplements — book ahead); drive-time guide (max practical day-drive with young children 2–3 hours; Tel Aviv to Eilat is 4h30 — long for toddlers; split with Masada stop); 7-day family circuit (Day 1: TLV arrival + beach; Day 2: Caesarea (amphitheatre + ancient port = history made fun) + Rosh Hanikra cable car (kid favourite); Day 3: Haifa cable car + Bahá'í Gardens (stroller-friendly) + Akko old city; Day 4: Sea of Galilee boat ride + Jesus Trail segment (short) + Tabgha; Day 5: Jordan River north swim spot + Bet She'an Roman ruins (kid-friendly open-air; minimal climbing); Day 6: Jerusalem family visit (Yad Vashem children's wing, City of David tunnel — kids LOVE the water walk, Old City scavenger hunt); Day 7: Dead Sea float (toddler note: under 3 not recommended in Dead Sea water due to eyes/mouth); pool hotel tier (Isrotel Dead Sea or Daroma Mitzpe Ramon rated for pools; full family-room availability; breakfast included — critical for families). HONESTY: Dead Sea float not recommended for children under 3 (magnesium-rich water hazardous if swallowed — research supports this precaution; note but do not fabricate specific age rules since no Israeli law specifies); Israeli car seat supplement is mandatory on most company contracts — never suggest renting without one. Affiliate CTAs: DiscoverCars car rental (primary, with child-seat note), Booking.com family hotels (secondary, filter by pool + family room). - [P2] (seo-content, M) Israel during Passover — visitor guide for tourists (/israel-passover-travel) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "passover.travel|israel.during.passover|travel.*passover|passover.*tourist|/israel-passover" against full BACKLOG.md + DONE.md returns ZERO matches (the samaritan-community-israel backlog item mentions the Samaritan Passover but as part of a cultural guide, not a tourist-planning guide). EXISTING COVERAGE: traveling-israel-jewish-holidays.md covers Passover in 2–3 lines; rosh-hashanah-in-israel.md (sibling standalone seasonal guide SHIPPED). COMPETITOR EVIDENCE: goisrael.com/passover, itraveljerusalem.com/passover-israel, chabad.org/passover-tours-israel all rank for "visiting Israel during Passover"; zero comprehensive English editorial travel guide exists. WHY VALUABLE: Passover is Israel's most significant national holiday (8 days in spring — 2027: April 13–20; dates shift annually per Hebrew calendar); a large segment of international visitors (both Jewish diaspora and secular tourists) plan trips around Passover and encounter confusing practical conditions; Christian pilgrims often plan Holy Land visits near Easter (which frequently overlaps with Passover). Very high search intent: "Can I travel to Israel during Passover?", "Is Israel open during Passover?", "What restaurants are open during Passover Israel?". Content: what Passover means for tourists (chametz / leavened bread products banned from many hotels and some restaurants for 8 days; supermarket chametz sections taped off or sold to a fictional non-Jewish owner for the holiday week; hotels required by law to serve only kosher-for-Passover food or close their kitchens); practical food impact (non-kosher restaurants open as usual in Tel Aviv — tourist largely unaffected; Jerusalem and religious areas more restricted; matzo everywhere — stock up for a culturally rich experience); Seder night (first 2 nights; some hotels offer public Seder dinners for tourists — book months ahead at hotels like King David Jerusalem, Inbal, Dan Tel Aviv; Chabad.org can arrange Seder invitations at local homes for solo travellers worldwide including Israel — genuinely useful resource); transportation during Passover (inter-city trains run; buses on modified schedule; car rental useful; airports operate normally); crowds and pricing (Passover = spring peak + Israeli school holiday = hotels at or near capacity; 200–300% price surge vs. off-peak; book 4–6 months ahead); Passover tourist experiences (Western Wall during Passover = birkat kohanim (priestly blessing) ceremony on Chol HaMoed — massive outdoor gathering; 50,000+ attending; deeply moving; not ticketed; Tel Aviv fun-fair at Ganei Yehoshua during Chol HaMoed — Israel's largest temporary amusement park, family experience); best Passover tourist activities (national parks free or reduced entry on certain holidays — verify annually at parks.org.il; Jerusalem seder evening walking tours; Jaffa artisan market on Chol HaMoed). HONESTY: Passover dates shift annually — always note "verify exact 2026/2027 dates at timeanddate.com/holiday/israel"; "hotels required by law to serve kosher-for-Passover food" is the legal requirement for kosher-certified properties — non-certified hotels may still serve chametz; the chametz law is complex; do not fabricate specific restaurant counts open or closed; Chabad Seder invitation is a genuine resource but travellers should contact well in advance. Affiliate CTAs: Booking.com Israel hotels with spring/Passover advance-booking hook, GYG Jerusalem Passover Seder walking tour CTA. Cross-links: traveling-israel-jewish-holidays, rosh-hashanah-in-israel, western-wall-guide, first-time-in-israel, is-israel-safe, shabbat-guide (similar practical-conditions format). SEO: "Israel during Passover tourist guide", "visiting Israel Passover", "Passover Israel what to know", "Israel Pesach tourist tips", "travel to Israel at Passover 2027". Sources: goisrael.com/passover-in-israel, itraveljerusalem.com/passover, chabad.org/passover (Seder finder), parks.org.il (holiday schedule), touristisrael.com/passover-in-israel. [iter405 research] status: ready

- [P3] (monetization, S) Eilat tax-free shopping guide (/eilat-shopping-guide) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 grep scan for "eilat.shopping|eilat.*tax.free.*guide|eilat.*shopping.*guide" against full BACKLOG.md + DONE.md returns ZERO P-item matches as a dedicated guide. WHY VALUABLE: Eilat operates as a VAT-exempt zone (17% savings vs mainland Israel on electronics, cosmetics, alcohol, jewelry, and sportswear) — one of the strongest practical differentiators for Eilat vs other Israeli cities; Israeli domestic tourists explicitly plan shopping trips to Eilat; international visitors find the savings meaningful on luxury goods and electronics. Clear monetization: Booking.com Eilat hotel links (shoppers stay overnight for day-long shopping + resort day). CONTENT: How the VAT exemption works (by law, Eilat Municipality Act; applies to retail; VAT refund forms not needed — purchased goods are automatically exempt); what qualifies (electronics, cosmetics, perfume, alcohol, jewelry, sportswear, clothing); what doesn't qualify (restaurant meals, most services, fuel); best shopping centers (Mall Hayam — largest Eilat mall; Shalom Center; King Solomon's Wharf retail cluster; Cinematheque courtyard boutiques); practical tips (bring receipt + passport ID for border electronics purchases; price-compare app; bring suitcase space); honest caveat: prices are not always cheaper than online deals — verify against Amazon/local prices for electronics specifically; the VAT saving is real but retail markup varies. AFFILIATE CTAs: Booking.com Eilat hotel CTA (overnight stay = more shopping time; primary) + Skyscanner TLV–ETH flights CTA (for mainland Israelis flying to Eilat for shopping). CROSS-LINKS: eilat-travel-guide.md, eilat-beach-guide.md (when built), israel-cost-budget.md, shopping-in-israel.md. ## iter425 additions (2026-07-10 RESEARCH)
- [P3] (seo-content, S) Israel vs Portugal destination comparison (/israel-vs-portugal) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep "israel-vs-portugal" against full BACKLOG.md + DONE.md = ZERO hits. Continues the proven comparison series (Turkey/Egypt/Jordan/Greece/Cyprus/France SHIPPED; Morocco/Dubai/Spain/Italy in backlog). Portugal is a natural pairing: both offer ancient history, Mediterranean coastline, excellent food scenes, warm climates, and are popular with the same budget-to-mid European traveller demographic. Portugal's "value for money" reputation makes the Israel comparison high-stakes for budget-conscious decision makers. - [P3] (seo-content, S) Israel vs Croatia destination comparison (/israel-vs-croatia) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep "israel-vs-croatia" against full BACKLOG.md + DONE.md = ZERO hits. Croatia is one of Europe's top-selling Mediterranean destinations; travellers comparing "Adriatic coast + ancient walled cities + warm weather + foodie scene" regularly shortlist both Israel (Akko/Caesarea/Old City Jerusalem) and Croatia (Dubrovnik/Split/Diocletian's Palace). The competition is direct: Dubrovnik Old City and Akko's Crusader old city are structurally similar UNESCO heritage experiences, making the comparison highly organic and SEO-resonant. Content: 10-criteria side-by-side table (beaches/sea quality, ancient history, cuisine, costs, nightlife, entry/safety, flight distance, climate window, unique factor, family-friendliness); decision matrix (Biblical depth → Israel; Game of Thrones / pure Mediterranean beach → Croatia; island-hopping → Croatia; solo adventure budget → Croatia slight edge; combined trip feasibility: ZAG–TLV ~2.5h direct); honest tone (Croatian tourism is older and more polished; Israel has additional security layer at entry; "neither is 'better' — depends on your priority"). 3 affiliate CTAs: Booking.com Israel hotels + GYG Israel private tour + Viator Jerusalem tour. 5–6 FAQs. Dense cross-links: israel-vs-greece, israel-vs-turkey, akko-acre-guide, israel-cost-budget. HONESTY: same standards as comparison series. - [P3] (seo-content+monetization, S) Levinsky Market Tel Aviv food guide (/levinsky-market-guide) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep "levinsky-market-guide|levinsky.*market.*guide" against BACKLOG.md + DONE.md = ZERO standalone P-items. shopping-in-israel.md (SHIPPED iter276) names "Levinsky spice market Tel Aviv" as a single line in its multi-market hub; israel-markets-guide is a P3 hub in backlog. Levinsky deserves its own dedicated guide: it's the Persian-Jewish spice market culture heart of South Tel Aviv and increasingly a food-tourism destination rivalling Carmel Market (which has /tel-aviv-carmel-market.md SHIPPED). The area centred on Levinsky Street in the Florentin neighbourhood is distinct from the Carmel Market tourist experience — it's primarily visited by culinary-focused travellers and diaspora visitors seeking saffron, sumac, basmati, dried Persian limes, halvah slabs, and pickled everything. Content: what Levinsky is (South TLV spice-and-deli strip, born as Persian-Jewish immigrant community market post-1950s, now food-tourism institution); key stops (Café Levinsky 41 — spice-counter café hybrid, one of TLV's most-Instagrammed food spots; Levinsky 30 for dried fruit + nuts; Hatikva Market adjacency); Florentin neighbourhood context (street art, independent fashion, very local TLV vibe — contrast with tourist-heavy Carmel); what to buy (saffron — Israeli-market price fraction of supermarket; zaatar blends; Persian dried limes; Turkish coffee ground to order; Georgian churchkhela; tahini in bulk); how to get there (Levinsky St runs near Allenby intersection; nearest light rail: Herzl/Allenby stop on Red Line, 8-min walk; or bus 39 from Dizengoff); best time to visit (Tue + Thu mornings for fresh stock; avoid Friday afternoon when vendors pack up; closed Shabbat); honest framing (area gentrifying but still local; some vendors Hebrew-only). 2 affiliate CTAs: GYG TLV food market tour CTA (includes Levinsky) + Viator TLV street-food tour. 5 FAQs. Cross-links: shopping-in-israel, tel-aviv-food-guide, tel-aviv-neighborhoods-guide, carmel-market-guide (tel-aviv-carmel-market), jaffa-flea-market (when exists). HONESTY: no fabricated vendor names beyond well-documented anchors; "hours and vendor mix change — verify before visiting". SEO: "Levinsky Market Tel Aviv guide", "Levinsky spice market Tel Aviv", "Florentin food market Tel Aviv", "Persian Jewish market Tel Aviv". Sources: iter425 research — secrettelaviv.com + timeout.co.il/tel-aviv Levinsky coverage confirmed. status: ready [iter425 research]
- [P3] (seo-content, S) Tel Aviv Tayelet beachfront promenade guide (/tel-aviv-tayelet) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep "tel-aviv-tayelet|tayelet-guide|tel-aviv-promenade" against BACKLOG.md + DONE.md = ZERO standalone P-items. Content: overview of the promenade layout (Jaffa Port south end → Banana Beach → Frischmann → Gordon → Hilton → Nordau → Reading north end; approximate section lengths; landmark anchors at each section); named beach clubs and their character (Gordon Beach = most popular tourist + locals mix; Hilton = dog beach + gay beach north corner; Banana Beach = south of Gordon, younger vibe; Gordon Pool = Olympic-size sea-water pool on the promenade — iconic; Metzitzim "No-Name" Beach = North TLV, more local); e-scooter and e-bike rental along the Tayelet (Bird, Lime, Tel-O-Fun city bike share — stations every 500m along the route; recommended for the full north-south traverse); sunset watch spots (Gordon breakwater; Jaffa Clocktower sea view; Reading park); accessibility (fully paved, flat, wheelchair-accessible along the entire length; narrower at Jaffa section over old port cobbles); timing (beach season June–September for swimming; Tayelet itself pleasant April–November; crowded weekends July–August — early morning for peace); 2 affiliate CTAs: Booking.com beachfront Tel Aviv hotels CTA + GYG TLV beach day tour CTA. 5 FAQs. - [P3] (seo-content, S) Israel vs Georgia (country) destination comparison (/israel-vs-georgia) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep "israel-vs-georgia|georgia.*comparison" against BACKLOG.md + DONE.md = ZERO hits. Georgia (the Caucasus country: Tbilisi, Batumi, wine country) has exploded as a destination in 2024–2026 — it's the breakout "budget-friendly ancient-city + wine + mountains" destination for exactly the European and Israeli traveller demographic that also considers Israel. Georgia is unique in this comparison series: it has a significant Israeli traveller community (Israelis are one of the top inbound visitor nationalities to Georgia; Tel Aviv–Tbilisi direct flights run multiple times daily), which means both angles work — Israelis comparing "Georgia as a getaway" AND international travellers weighing Tbilisi vs Tel Aviv. Content: 10-criteria comparison table (cities + nightlife, ancient history, food + wine culture, costs, natural landscapes, safety, visa ease, flight distance from Europe, climate, unique factor); decision matrix (Biblical history → Israel; wine trail + mountain trekking → Georgia; budget city break → Georgia clear winner; Mediterranean beach → Israel; combined trip: TLV–TBS ~3h = very easy 2-destination trip); honest framing (Georgia has no sea; Israel has no Caucasus Mountains; cuisines are both underrated globally — "hidden gem" angle works for both); honesty: safety note for both countries' respective border regions without fearmongering — factual gov-advisory links. 3 affiliate CTAs: Booking.com Israel hotels + GYG Israel tours + DiscoverCars Israel rental. 5 FAQs. Cross-links: israel-vs-greece, israel-vs-turkey (Caucasus mention), israel-cost-budget, first-time-in-israel. HONESTY: Georgian lari / Israeli shekel rate changes — no hardcoded conversion; safety framing uses gov-advisory-link approach not personal assessment. SEO: "Israel or Georgia vacation", "Israel vs Georgia travel 2026", "Tbilisi or Tel Aviv trip", "should I visit Israel or Georgia". Sources: iter425 research — confirmed zero prior backlog+done hits; Lonely Planet + NomadGate Georgia trend articles confirmed. status: ready [iter425 research]

- **`/israel-for-indian-travelers`** | P3 | M | seo-content+monetization | [iter430 research]
  CRITICAL DISTINCTION: Indian passport holders are NOT on Israel's ETA-IL visa-exempt list — they require a full visa. The eVisa-B2 system launched July 1 2025 by the Israeli Embassy in New Delhi (apply via embassies.gov.il/india; 3-week minimum processing; ₪50 fee; 98% approval rate for tourism). This makes the guide significantly more complex than the S-effort ETA-IL guides. Additional content: El Al direct Delhi–TLV route, IST = UTC+5:30 (Israel is ~90 min behind IST in summer — minimal jet lag), vegetarian/vegan food options at Israeli restaurants (important for many Indian travelers), kosher restaurant overlap for vegetarians, growing Christian pilgrimage market from Kerala/Tamil Nadu/Goa (Nazareth/Bethlehem circuit), cash vs UPI note (UPI not accepted; Mastercard/Visa widely accepted). CTAs: Booking.com + GYG pilgrimage tour + DiscoverCars. 6 FAQs (visa process, halal food, best time, safety, budget, SIM card). Smoke test route. NOTE: effort is M (not S) due to visa complexity requiring accurate research.

- [P3] (seo-content+monetization, M) 3-day Golan Heights itinerary (/3-days-in-golan) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep for "3-days-in-golan|golan.*3.day.itinerary" in BACKLOG+DONE returns zero P-item standalone entries (1 hit = description within SHIPPED northern-israel-road-trip which covers Golan as 1 day of a multi-region 4-day drive). DISTINCT from northern-israel-road-trip (multi-region 4-day circuit; Golan = 1 day only). WHY VALUABLE: Golan Heights is a standalone 3-day destination with its own distinct character (volcanic basalt landscape, Druze villages, wineries, nature reserves); travelers specifically searching "3 days in Golan Heights" or "Golan weekend" find only tour operators and AI aggregators, not editorial content. Audience: families, wine enthusiasts, nature hikers, history/archaeology travelers (Gamla). CONTENT: Day 1: drive from TLV/Haifa → Katzrin (capital of the Golan; Ancient Katzrin Archaeological Park — 2nd century CE village + synagogue; Golan Winery tasting; Bental wine + gourmet restaurant); afternoon: Mount Bental observation point (abandoned Syrian military bunkers + lookout to Damascus plain; powerful Cold War + recent Syria civil war context — frame carefully); overnight: kibbutz guesthouse (Ein Zivan / Neve Ativ / Merom Golan; kibbutz character; different from urban hotel). Day 2: Banias Nature Reserve (most powerful spring in Israel; Banias waterfalls — most photogenic; Tel Dan Nature Reserve alternative / combine; ancient Banias/Caesarea Philippi excavations — New Testament context: "Upon this rock I will build my church"); afternoon: Nimrod Fortress National Park (finest Crusader/Ayyubid castle in Israel; commanding views of Hermon + Galilee; 30-min uphill walk; INPA pass valid); Druze village dinner in Majdal Shams (excellent Druze cuisine: kibbeh, maftoul; Druze hospitality; Apple Hill — Druze organic apple orchards). Day 3: Gamla Nature Reserve (Late Second Temple Jewish community that resisted Roman siege 67 CE; vulture colony = one of largest griffon vulture nesting sites in Israel; dramatic canyon hike 3–4h moderate; Gamla waterfall; INPA pass valid) → return via Tiberias for Sea of Galilee lunch. HONESTY: Mount Bental is close to the Syrian border — always check current gov.il advisory for Golan area before visit; never enter security zones near the boundary; Banias waterfalls may have reduced flow in late summer (link INPA for seasonal updates); Druze community is welcoming to tourists but frame respectfully (not an anthropological exhibit); Gamla massacre in 67 CE is a historically sobering site — frame with appropriate gravity. CTAs: DiscoverCars affiliate (car essential) + Booking.com kibbutz guesthouses Golan CTA + GYG "Golan Heights jeep tour" affiliate. Cross-links: golan-heights-guide.md + golan-heights-tours-compared.md + druze-villages-carmel.md + northern-israel-road-trip.md + nimrod-fortress-guide.md. SEO: "3 days in Golan Heights", "Golan Heights weekend itinerary", "Golan Israel 3 day trip". Sources: parks.org.il/gamla, parks.org.il/nimrod-fortress, parks.org.il/banias, golan-winery.com. [iter440 research] status: ready

- [P3] (seo-content+monetization, S) Eilat vs Hurghada: Red Sea comparison guide (/eilat-vs-hurghada) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep scan for "eilat.*hurghada|hurghada.*eilat|red.sea.*compar.*guide|eilat-vs-hurghada" in BACKLOG.md + DONE.md returns ZERO P-item matches. WHY VALUABLE: "Eilat vs Hurghada" is a real traveler decision query; TripAdvisor forum threads for "Eilat Hurghada or Aqaba" confirm active demand from UK/European winter sun seekers — yet NO editorial travel publisher has a standalone comparison guide. Distinct from israel-vs-egypt.md (SHIPPED — country-level comparison covering history/food/culture; does NOT address the specific Red Sea beach/diving holiday decision). THIS guide targets: travelers who want a Red Sea beach/dive holiday and are choosing between the Israeli and Egyptian options. CONTENT: 10-criteria side-by-side comparison (diving quality: Eilat — Coral Beach Nature Reserve, BNEI controlled access, reef-safe sunscreen mandatory, approx 350 coral species + Red Sea fish; Hurghada — El Gouna area dive sites, more pelagic diversity especially open-water, generally considered more spectacular dive sites for experienced divers; diving infrastructure: both have strong dive operators; cost: Hurghada all-inclusive resort strip typically $80–160/night for 4-star; Eilat hotel prices higher by ~30–50% due to Israeli cost structure; flight options: direct London→Eilat El Al + charter; London→Hurghada via TUI + Thomas Cook charter direct; Petra day-trip: ONLY from Eilat — 30-min drive to Yitzhak Rabin crossing, Petra = most popular add-on and a unique Eilat advantage; Hurghada has no major historical site within a day trip; safety/entry: Eilat ETA-IL; Egypt requires tourist visa but grants on arrival for most EU/UK/US passports at Hurghada airport; Sinai clarification CRITICAL: Hurghada is on the WESTERN Egyptian Red Sea coast — NOT in Sinai; UK FCDO + EU advisories warn against Sinai travel but Hurghada is specifically EXEMPT from these Sinai warnings and generally considered safe for resort tourism; NEVER conflate Hurghada with Sinai in copy; resort style: Eilat = city-resort hybrid (Israeli beach town with restaurants, bars, shopping + beach strip; visitors mix with Israeli families; lively city feel); Hurghada = dedicated resort strip more separated from local life; for couples seeking pure resort, Hurghada may suit; for travelers wanting culture alongside beach, Eilat + Petra package wins; coral reef condition: both reefs under environmental pressure; Eilat INPA enforcement (reef-safe sunscreen + depth limits) is one of the best managed coral reefs globally; Hurghada reef has suffered from anchor damage historically; climate: both 22–26°C Jan–Feb; Eilat arguably marginally warmer in winter due to geography; nightlife: Eilat has proper city nightlife (bars, clubs on Dekel Beach strip); Hurghada has resort-strip entertainment; neither is Ibiza). HONESTY: Hurghada vs Sinai distinction is CRITICAL — UK FCDO advises against Sinai (including Sharm El-Sheikh) but Hurghada is NOT under that advisory; always link relevant government advisory; reef-safe sunscreen mandatory at Eilat's Coral Beach (INPA rule); never declare one "better" overall — frame as different experiences for different traveler profiles; no fabricated hotel prices — use ranges; Egyptian visa-on-arrival confirmed accurate as of research; verify at embassy before publication. AFFILIATE CTAs: Booking.com Eilat hotels (primary) + GYG Eilat reef snorkel + Skyscanner flights to Eilat vs Hurghada comparison. CROSS-LINKS: eilat-travel-guide.md, eilat-diving-snorkeling.md, israel-vs-egypt.md, petra-from-eilat-vs-amman.md, red-canyon-eilat.md. 6 FAQs (Hurghada vs Sinai safety distinction; which has better diving; Petra day trip logistics; budget comparison; can I combine both; best season). SEO: "Eilat vs Hurghada", "Eilat or Hurghada holiday", "Red Sea Israel Egypt comparison 2026", "Eilat Hurghada which is better". Sources: tripadvisor.com/ShowTopic Eilat Hurghada Aqaba (forum threads confirm demand), travelsupermarket.com/red-sea (market data), eilat.city/en (official tourism), UK FCDO Egypt advice (fcdo.gov.uk), bneiisrael.co.il.
