- [P3] (seo-content, S) Midbarium Desert Animal Park Beersheba guide (/midbarium-beersheba-guide) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep scan for "midbarium", "negev zoo", "beersheba.*animal.*park", "mandel.*animal.*park" across full BACKLOG.md + DONE.md returns ZERO matches; no file at src/content/guides/midbarium-beersheba-guide.md. WHY VALUABLE: Midbarium (official: Jack, Joseph and Morton Mandel Animal Park) opened 2024 in Beer Sheva, replacing the old Negev Zoo (demolished 2022); 140-dunam (37-acre) interactive desert zoo with 100+ species including three rare white lions, two hippos, giraffes, zebras, meerkats, Nile crocodiles, endangered Guinea baboons; 15 signature interactive experiences (See Like a Raptor, Climb Like an Ibex, Sneak Up Like a Leopard, Slither Like a Snake, Navigate Like a Bat, Ambush Like a Crocodile, Romp Like a Monkey, etc.); four desert habitat biomes (canyon, prairie, oasis, savannah); one of Israel's newest and most significant family attractions; israel-in-photos.com has standalone guide; Jerusalem Post covered opening; zero competitor Israel travel editorial has a dedicated guide. CONTENT: overview (midbar = Hebrew for desert; interactive zoo where visitors "move like the animals" rather than passive viewing; four biome habitats with animals placed in naturalistic desert enclosures); highlights (three white lions — photographed attraction; hippo pool; giraffe feeding area; meerkat burrow viewing; raptor aviaries; crocodile habitat; Guinea baboon troop); 15 interactive challenges (physical experiences integrated with animal observation zones: raptor-vision tunnel, ibex climbing wall, leopard stealth crawl, bat echolocation game, etc.; excellent for children 5+); visiting info (Beer Sheva western entrance near Route 25; opening hours Sunday–Thursday 09:00–17:30, holidays/Saturdays 09:00–17:00, Friday + holiday eves 09:00–15:00; entry closes 2h before closing; 4h typical visit; kiosk at entrance; restrooms throughout; verify wheelchair access via midbarium.org.il; advance booking recommended weekends + school holidays); getting there (15–20 min by bus/taxi from Beer Sheva Central Station or Beersheba North (Ramot) train station; Beer Sheva is 1h10 direct from Tel Aviv HaShalom ₪42 approx; check Moovit for current bus routing); Beer Sheva full-day combo (Midbarium 4h afternoon + Tel Be'er Sheva UNESCO site or Thursday Bedouin market morning; Israel Air Force Museum optional add-on Tue–Sat). HONESTY: opening hours from 2024–2026 opening data — always link midbarium.org.il; white lion count from official Mandel Foundation press release (3 white lions) — do not elaborate; "interactive experiences" count of 15 per official JPost/Mandel Foundation sources; admission prices link only (no hardcode); Midbarium is NOT a large-scale safari park like Ramat Gan — manage expectations (desert species focus, not full African safari scale). Dense cross-links: beer-sheva-guide (P3 BACKLOG — natural companion; bidirectional cross-link), negev region hub, south-israel-itinerary, israel-with-kids, israel-road-trip-with-kids. Monetization: Booking.com Beer Sheva hotel CTA + GYG southern Israel day tour CTA. SEO: "Midbarium Beersheba guide", "Beer Sheva animal park Israel", "Midbarium Israel 2026 visitor guide", "interactive zoo Israel", "new zoo Beer Sheva", "things to do Beer Sheva Israel". ## iter480 additions (2026-07-12 RESEARCH — saturation check; 3 net-new items)

- [P3] (seo-content+monetization, S) Israel kitesurfing guide (/israel-kitesurfing) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep for "kitesurfing\|kitesurf\|kite.surf\|kite-surf\|kite board\|kiteboard" across full BACKLOG+DONE returns ZERO P-item matches (2 passing mentions within israel-adventure-sports.md "kitesurfing (Haifa Bay/Eilat)" and egypt-from-israel "kite-surfing offshore" — NOT standalone coverage). DISTINCT from: israel-surfing.md (SHIPPED iter472 — Mediterranean wave surfing; different beaches, skill set, equipment, and community); israel-adventure-sports.md (SHIPPED — covers 12+ sports in 1-2 sentences each; no kitesurfing depth). WHY VALUABLE: Kitesurfing is a fast-growing watersport with a dedicated global community; "Israel kitesurfing" + "kitesurfing Eilat" + "kite school Israel" have solid long-tail search volume; Tourist Israel has no dedicated kite page; multiple specialist directories cover Israel (kitejungle.com/kite-places/israel, unplug-kitesurf.com/destinations/israel, globalkitespots.com/global-kitesurf-spots-and-regions/israel, wannakitesurf.com). High affiliate potential: IKO-certified lessons (GYG "kitesurfing lesson Israel"), DiscoverCars rental base. CONTENT: 5 main spots — (1) Herzliya: Ha Sharon Beach (flat water, steady thermal wind, beginner-friendly, main kite school hub, IKS school); (2) Haifa: Bat Galim / Dado Beach (less crowded, Bat Galim designated kite zone); (3) Tel Aviv south: Ashdod Beach (Alin Club, south of Tel Aviv, flat sandy launch, shallow water); (4) Netanya: Poleg Beach / Windrose school (open swell plus flat-water option); (5) Eilat: North Beach / Coral Beach (Red Sea thermal wind, year-round season — best Nov–Apr Mediterranean off-season, calmer water than Mediterranean, Kite X, Surf Center Eilat, Kite Master Eilat). Seasons: Mediterranean coast — May–September "Erez" thermal winds (north-northwest; consistent 15–25 knots by afternoon; mornings lighter); Eilat — year-round, peak Nov–Apr; winter storms Oct = Mediterranean flat-water window. Gear: IKO course structure (12-hour learn-to-kite; ₪1,800–2,400 for 3-session course); equipment rental ~₪200/h; who to contact: KDI.org.il = Israel Kitesurfing Federation (national governing body; school directory). HONESTY: Herzliya Ha Sharon Beach can get crowded July–August; Eilat's Red Sea kitesurfing is distinct from its coral reef conservation area (kite launch away from coral); "flat water" at Herzliya = inside harbour breakwater, not all of Ha Sharon Beach — check local conditions; wind forecast apps (Windfinder, Windy). Affiliate CTAs: GYG "kitesurfing lesson Israel" search CTA + DiscoverCars (car to reach kite spots outside TLV) + Booking.com Eilat hotels CTA (Red Sea kite base). Cross-links: israel-surfing (wave surfing contrast), eilat-travel-guide (Red Sea base), israel-adventure-sports, herzliya-guide (if built). SEO: "Israel kitesurfing guide", "kitesurfing Eilat Red Sea", "kite school Israel", "kitesurfing Herzliya", "Israel kitesurfing spots". Sources: kitejungle.com/kite-places/israel, unplug-kitesurf.com/destinations/israel, globalkitespots.com/global-kitesurf-spots-and-regions/israel, kdi.org.il/en/info/shops/schools, eilat.city/en/kite-x, eilat.city/en/surf-center, eilat.city/en/kite-master, tripadvisor.com/Attractions-g293980-Activities-c61-t197-Eilat_Southern_District.html. - [P3] (seo-content, S) Traveling to Israel while pregnant (/israel-pregnancy-travel) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep for "pregnant\|pregnancy\|maternity\|expectant mother\|trimester" across full BACKLOG+DONE returns ZERO matches. WHY VALUABLE: Pregnant travelers are a high-anxiety planning segment actively seeking reassurance; Tripadvisor Israel forum has active threads ("Israel while pregnant", "Visiting Israel whilst pregnant"); no dedicated guide on any major Israel travel site. Zero ENGLISH-language resource specifically for pregnant travelers to Israel. CONTENT: Trimester guidance (1st: miscarriage risk = avoid remote hiking; 2nd = safest window; 3rd = check airline policies before booking; El Al, EasyJet, Wizz Air all require medical clearance at 28+ weeks — exact policies differ); Dead Sea: safe for most pregnant women (floating is low-impact; HIGH SALT = avoid ingestion + eye contact + cuts; heat risk — cap to 30 min max in summer; avoid ₪200+ mud wraps if skin is broken); Israeli healthcare quality (Hadassah Ein Kerem = world-class NICU and obstetrics; Tel Aviv Sourasky Medical Centre; all hospitals equipped to world standard; EHIC/travel insurance essential — as per any international travel); Airport security: body scanners at Ben Gurion = low-dose X-ray (IATA-approved as safe for pregnancy; alternative pat-down always available on request); security screening interview = standard regardless of pregnancy; Zika status: Israel has NO Zika transmission (confirmed by WHO — distinct from many warm destinations); Heat precautions (summer in Dead Sea region = 40°C+; Negev spring-autumn recommended for pregnant visitors); Food safety: Israeli restaurant standards are high; avoid raw fish (not common in Israeli cuisine); hummus and falafel = low risk; listeria risk from soft cheeses (brie, camembert; less common in Israeli diet); kosher dairy labelling ("chalav Yisrael") = safe pasteurised; Hiking limitations: flat sites suitable all trimesters (Caesarea, Tel Aviv, Capernaum); low-elevation hikes (Ein Gedi lower trails in first 6 months); avoid Masada Snake Path in summer + 3rd trimester; Jordan River Baptism Site (flat ground, safe); Water activities: Mediterranean swimming fine (avoid strong surf; pack non-slip sandals for rocky entries); Dead Sea floating is the ideal pregnant-traveller activity (no physical exertion; unique gravity-free experience). HONESTY: This is informational, not medical advice — always consult the traveler's own OB/GYN before travel; Israeli healthcare is excellent but travel insurance is mandatory; premature birth in a foreign country is expensive and stressful. Cross-links: dead-sea-guide, ben-gurion-airport-guide, israel-accessible-travel (similar planning mindset), best-time-to-visit-israel. SEO: "traveling to Israel while pregnant", "Israel pregnancy travel guide", "Dead Sea pregnant safe", "Israel trip pregnant", "visiting Israel first trimester". Sources: Tripadvisor Israel forum ("Israel while pregnant" threads), iata.org (body scanner safety in pregnancy), who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news (Israel Zika status), travel.state.gov/israel. [iter480 research] status: ready

- [P3] (seo-content+monetization, S) Emmaus Trail hiking guide (/emmaus-trail-guide) — CONFIRMED GAP: grep for "emmaus.trail\|emmaus-trail\|trail.*emmaus" across full BACKLOG+DONE returns ZERO P-item matches ("emmaus" found only twice: both referring to Castrum Emmaus = Latrun Monastery Crusader fortress — a tourist site, NOT the hiking trail). DISTINCT from: israel-national-trail (1,000km INT; different trail entirely), jesus-trail (65km Galilee trail Nazareth → Capernaum; northern Israel; BACKLOG P2), christian-pilgrimage-tour-guide (overview of Christian sites; not trail-specific), hiking-in-israel (overview of trails; does not feature Emmaus Trail). WHY VALUABLE: The Emmaus Trail is a 20km signposted pilgrimage hiking route through the Judean Hills — inaugurated recently, well-documented, growing in Christian pilgrim and hiking circles. Coverage: biblicalarchaeology.org, israel21c.org, secret-israel.com, saxum.org (official trailhead + guidebook), Abraham Tours ("Jesus Trail" package nearby). High affiliate potential: Christian pilgrim segment (GYG/Viator Christian tour CTAs; Booking.com Abu Ghosh accommodation). CONTENT: Route overview (trailhead: Saxum Visitor Center, Abu Ghosh — the Crusader-era identification of Biblical Emmaus; ends: Emmaus Nicopolis near Latrun; ~20km / 12.5 miles; 5–6h at moderate pace; well-waymarked blue-white trail markers; follows approximate ancient Roman road Jerusalem → Jaffa); Saxum Visitor Center (multimedia museum dedicated to life of Jesus; recommended 1h before setting off; entrance fee ~₪60; must pre-book; saxum.org); Abu Ghosh village (Israel's informal hummus capital; stop for breakfast/lunch; recommended restaurants: Abu Ghosh Restaurant, Caravan Restaurant, Carmela's; 12km west of Jerusalem via Route 1; buses 185/186 from Jerusalem Central); Trail highlights — Olive groves and Judean Hills views; Byzantine-era mosaics at Emmaus Nicopolis (entrance fee); Latrun Monastery at trail end (wine + olive oil shop; Cistercian monks; free to visit shop Mon–Sat 8am–5:30pm); practical logistics (one-way trail: take bus 185/186 from Jerusalem to Abu Ghosh; return from Latrun via bus/taxi to Jerusalem ~40 min; car parking at Saxum; water carry 2L; best season March–May and Oct–Nov; summer hiking at risk of heat; trail is waymarked but download map from saxum.org/emmaus-trail in case of poor signal); HONESTY: The trail passes through varied terrain including some private agricultural land near field paths — follow waymarks; Emmaus Nicopolis site condition varies — check ahead at franciscan.org for opening hours; trail is 20km and not suitable for casual walkers — appropriate footwear required. Affiliate CTAs: GYG "Christian hiking tour Israel" or "Judean Hills hike" CTA + Booking.com Abu Ghosh accommodation CTA + DiscoverCars (drive to trailhead). Cross-links: abu-ghosh (no standalone guide — cross-link to day-trips-from-jerusalem), latrun-monastery-guide (if built), christian-pilgrimage-guide, hiking-in-israel, israel-hidden-gems, day-trips-from-jerusalem. SEO: "Emmaus Trail Israel hiking guide", "Emmaus Trail Abu Ghosh", "Christian hiking trail Israel", "Emmaus Trail pilgrimage walk", "hiking from Abu Ghosh Israel". Sources: saxum.org/emmaus-trail, biblicalarchaeology.org/daily/biblical-sites-places/the-emmaus-trail, israel21c.org/hike-in-jesuss-footsteps-along-the-emmaus-trail, secret-israel.com/travel/hike-in-jesuss-footsteps-along-the-emmaus-trail. EXISTING COVERAGE: jewish-genealogy-israel-trip.md (SHIPPED iter467, ANU genealogy + Yad Vashem research angle); jewish-heritage-israel.md (SHIPPED — general Jewish heritage guide); christian-pilgrimage-holy-land.md (SHIPPED — multi-faith pilgrimage). NONE covers the specific Poland-to-Israel narrative journey format. WHY VALUABLE: The "Roots & Resilience" / "March of the Living" travel format is a distinct, high-intent product sold by JNF, Ramah, Birthright Extensions, Jewish Heritage Travel. Combines Holocaust memorial sites in Poland (Auschwitz-Birkenau, Krakow Kazimierz, Warsaw Ghetto area) with Israel as the "Jewish renaissance" conclusion chapter. Competitors: jnf.org/travel/tour/index/roots-resilience (organized program), ramah.org.il/rii/poland-journey, jhtravel.org (Jewish Heritage Travel Poland+Israel), polandjewishheritagetours.com — none produce a standalone editorial planning guide. Zero standalone Israel travel editorial addresses "what to see in Israel after the Poland memorial experience" as a trip design problem. CONTENT: The narrative arc explained (Holocaust → State of Israel: why the pairing is emotionally meaningful); structural options: (a) organized programs (JNF Roots & Resilience, Ramah Poland Journey, March of the Living extension — note apply months ahead, link organizer websites, not a hardcoded itinerary price); (b) independent design (Warsaw or Kraków → TLV via LOT Airlines direct, 2h40m); Poland chapter (key sites: Auschwitz-Birkenau, Kraków Kazimierz Jewish Quarter, Schindler's Factory Museum, Warsaw Ghetto Memorial, Treblinka — framed for Israel-bound travelers, not Poland-centric guide); Israel chapter (sites that specifically deepen the Poland narrative — Yad Vashem Holocaust History Museum advance reservation mandatory; Ghetto Fighters' House Museum at Kibbutz Lohamei HaGeta'ot near Akko — world's first Holocaust museum, founded 1949 by survivors; Buchenwalt Kibbutz memorial near Haifa; Givat HaMoreh Survivors' Park near Afula — lesser-known landscape of memory; Masada as defiance symbol; Ben-Gurion's Sde Boker retreat — the human who made Israel possible); suggested 7-day Israel extension after a Poland week; working with a guide vs. independent; emotional preparation notes (Yad Vashem + Auschwitz same trip = intense; recommendation to pace it); practical logistics (visa: most Western passports = ETA-IL; LOT Airlines Kraków→TLV and Warsaw→TLV direct; Jerusalem or Tel Aviv base for the Israel chapter). HONESTY: organized program details change annually — link organizer sites, never hardcode prices or itineraries; Ghetto Fighters' House Museum hours + admission should be verified at bet-lohamei-haghetaot.com; Yad Vashem advance booking required (yad-vashem.org) — call this out clearly; do NOT overstate the emotional experience or prescribe how travelers should feel; both countries have ongoing political complexity — frame with accuracy and care. Affiliate CTAs: TourRadar (Poland+Israel multi-day heritage packages), GYG (Yad Vashem private guided tours), Booking.com (Jerusalem hotels near Yad Vashem — Givat Ram/Bayit VeGan area). Cross-links: jewish-heritage-israel.md (new paragraph linking to /poland-israel-heritage-journey), jewish-genealogy-israel-trip.md (brief mention of Poland→Israel format in the planning section), christian-pilgrimage-holy-land.md (acknowledge multi-faith visitors on Poland-Israel programs). SEO: "Poland Israel heritage journey", "Holocaust memorial Israel trip", "March of Living Israel extension", "roots resilience Israel", "Poland Israel combined trip", "Auschwitz Yad Vashem same trip". Sources: jnf.org, ramah.org.il, jhtravel.org, polandjewishheritagetours.com, bet-lohamei-haghetaot.com. [iter485 research] status: ready

WHY VALUABLE: Jerusalem's meditative spiritual atmosphere paired with the growing third-wave specialty coffee scene creates a distinctive travel hook different from Tel Aviv's café culture. High-intent query "best coffee in Jerusalem" — competitor coverage strong: touristisrael.com "Best Coffee in Jerusalem 2026", debbestfood.com "Guide to Best Restaurants and Street Food in Jerusalem" (coffee section), thecoffeevine.com Jerusalem café guide, tabletmag.com Jerusalem coffee culture feature. We have zero dedicated Jerusalem coffee page. CONTENT: Opening hook: Jerusalem's coffee culture sits at the intersection of the sacred and the artisanal; kosher-certified specialty cafés are rarer than TLV but higher atmosphere-to-cup ratio; key cafés (cross-check opening hours on Google before authoring — cafés open/close frequently): Power CoffeeWorks (Mahane Yehuda roaster, open early for market shoppers), Shuk Kaffe (inside the shuk, tight seating, single-origin pour-overs), Birma (Emek Refaim Street, Jerusalem award-winning specialty café, Shabbat-closed), Tmol Shilshom (130-year-old stone building + independent bookstore, fully kosher, Hebrew literature-themed), Sybaris Rehavia (cold-brew specialist, neighbourhood café), Cafelix (Jerusalem branch of the TLV pioneer roaster), Coffee Mill (single-origin beans, counter service, neighbourhood institution); area guide: German Colony / Emek Refaim (Birma + café strip, quiet residential street; best morning atmosphere), Mahane Yehuda market (Power CoffeeWorks + Shuk Kaffe; best on Thursday/Friday morning bustle; market transforms into bar scene Fri evening — cafés close early), Mamilla / Jaffa Gate area (café options within walking distance of Old City walls); practical notes: many Jerusalem cafés are kosher (closed Fri sunset–Sat night); halav Yisrael milk standard common; advance check of Shabbat closure hours critical; cold brew preferred by many local baristas in summer heat (city is 700–800m elevation; cooler than TLV but not cool); affiliate CTAs: GYG Mahane Yehuda food tour CTA (morning market walk passes the coffee stalls; logical cross-sell) + Booking.com Jerusalem hotel CTA; cross-links: mahane-yehuda-market-guide, jerusalem-food-guide, jerusalem-travel-guide, tel-aviv-coffee-guide (reciprocal "in Tel Aviv" paragraph), israel-food-tours-cooking-classes. HONESTY: Do NOT fabricate ratings, prices, or "best in Israel" superlatives; link to each café's Google Maps listing for live hours; note Shabbat closures upfront; many shops close Rosh Chodesh mornings. - [P3] (seo-content, S) Krav Maga experience guide for Israel tourists (/krav-maga-israel-experience) — CONFIRMED GAP: python3 regex scan for "krav", "krav.maga", "martial.art", "self.defense.*israel" across full BACKLOG.md + DONE.md + src/content/guides/ returns ZERO matches. DISTINCT from israel-adventure-sports.md (SHIPPED — no Krav Maga content inside); distinct from private-tours-israel.md. WHY VALUABLE: Krav Maga is an IDF-developed combat discipline invented by Imi Lichtenfeld in Israel; tourist demand exists for "try Krav Maga in Israel"; competitor coverage: kravmagaisrael.com (7-day immersion camp), kravmaga-ikmf.com (IKMF crash camp; "Train in Israel" page), your-krav-maga-expert.com/krav-maga-tour-train-israel, tripaneer.com/all/c/krav-maga-camps/d/israel (12-day camp), TripAdvisor "Krav Maga Israel" dedicated listing in TLV. High-intent niche with zero editorial coverage on any mainstream Israel travel site. CONTENT: what Krav Maga is (IDF close-combat system; developed by Imi Lichtenfeld 1940s–1960s in Slovakia → Haifa; adopted by IDF 1948; Israel = origin country + globally recognised best place to train); experience options (1. Tourist taster class: 2–3h beginner-friendly session in TLV or Jerusalem; operators: Krav Maga Israel (kravmagaisrael.com), IKMF World Centre (kravmaga-ikmf.com), GYG/Viator bookable classes; no fitness level required; ~₪200–400/person; 2. Multi-day camps: IKMF Crash Camp 5 days; kravmagaisrael.com 7-day immersion ~$1,500–2,500 all-in; Tripaneer 12-day variant; 3. Drop-in self-defense classes at TLV gyms for longer stays); logistics (TLV gyms concentrated in south TLV / Florentin; IKMF World Centre in Netanya; dress: comfortable exercise clothes + closed-toe shoes; no equipment needed for tourist classes); Imi Lichtenfeld grave (Netanya cemetery — niche pilgrim point for practitioners); HONESTY: "train with IDF experts" = retired IDF-trained coaches, NOT active-duty soldiers; tourist taster classes are minimal-contact (not combat; more choreography + technique drill); Krav Maga is not a competitive sport (no tournaments; purely self-defense); quality varies significantly by school — verify instructor credentials; class dynamics differ from martial arts schools in other countries. Affiliate CTAs: GYG "Krav Maga class Israel" or "self-defense Israel" CTA (primary); Viator CTA (secondary). Cross-links: israel-adventure-sports, first-time-in-israel, tel-aviv-neighborhoods-guide.
